macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Plunge as Iran Shuts Strait of Hormuz, Brent Crude Hits Two-Week High
items.summary: Major US indexes fell sharply after Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a 3% drop in the Nasdaq Composite and a sell-off in big tech; Brent crude rose to a two-week high while travel stocks like United Airlines dropped 3% amid rising fuel costs.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Testifies Tuesday as Inflation Data and Bank Earnings Loom
items.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is scheduled to testify before Congress Tuesday morning just after June CPI data releases, while major banks prepare to report quarterly results; Fed officials remain divided on US inflation views with home prices hitting all-time highs.

items.title: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 3% Citing Iran War Energy Shock
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund downgraded its 2026 world economy outlook to 3% growth, citing the energy shock from the Iran war, though AI demand is expected to partly offset the impact.

items.title: SK Hynix US IPO Shares Slip Nearly 8% After Record-Breaking $26.5B Nasdaq Debut
items.summary: South Korean chip giant SK Hynix completed the largest-ever foreign firm IPO in the US at $26.5 billion, eclipsing Alibaba's 2014 record, but its Nasdaq-listed shares fell nearly 8% as debut euphoria cooled.

items.title: Trump Announces 20% Hormuz Toll and US Maritime Blockade on Iran Starting Tuesday
items.summary: President Trump plans to charge a 20% toll on ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz and will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on Iran on Tuesday, escalating tensions that have already shaken markets and boosted oil prices.

items.title: Fed's Waller Flags Possible Rate Hike in 'Near Term' Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller indicated higher interest rates may be needed in the near term as US inflation topped the RBI aim after 17 months, with escalating US-Iran tensions boosting oil prices and raising inflation concerns globally.

items.title: Korean Stocks Sink as AI Chip Selloff Sparks Trading Halt, KOSPI Drops 8.95%
items.summary: Korean markets experienced a sharp decline with the KOSPI falling 8.95% to 6,806.93 and KOSDAQ dropping 4.55% as an AI chip selloff triggered a trading halt, reflecting broader tech weakness spreading from Asia to the US.

items.title: US Oil Reserves Hit Lowest Levels Since 1983 Amid Iran Tensions and Supply Concerns
items.summary: US oil reserves have fallen to their lowest levels since 1983 as growing tensions with Iran and global supply disruptions, including disrupted Qatari supplies, drive up demand for American LNG cargoes.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Crashes Below $62,000 and Ethereum Drops to $1,750 Amid US-Iran Conflict
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below the critical $62,000 level and Ethereum approached $1,750 as escalating US-Iran tensions triggered $377 million in global crypto liquidations affecting nearly 90,000 investors.

items.title: Coinbase Lays Off 700 Employees, Cutting 14% of Staff
items.summary: Major crypto exchange Coinbase is reducing its workforce by approximately 14%, cutting around 700 employees in a significant industry contraction.

items.title: Polymarket Refunds Users After Hackers Steal Crypto via Compromised Vendor
items.summary: Prediction market platform Polymarket announced refunds for affected users after a third-party vendor compromise injected malicious scripts into its frontend, leading to a crypto theft.

items.title: Block Launches New Crypto Wallet with OLED Touchscreen Display
items.summary: Jack Dorsey's Block has released a follow-up to its Bitkey wallet, featuring an OLED touchscreen that allows users to verify transactions and authorize security measures directly.

items.title: Chainalysis Report: Russia, Iran, North Korea Moved $104B in Crypto to Bypass Sanctions
items.summary: A new Chainalysis report reveals sanctioned entities in Russia, Iran, and North Korea conducted an estimated $104 billion in crypto transactions in 2025 to evade Western sanctions, with North Korea stealing over $2 billion in digital assets.

items.title: Trust Wallet Reports Security Incident Affecting Chrome Extension Version 2.68
items.summary: Trust Wallet announced a security incident impacting version 2.68 of its Chrome extension, prompting users to take immediate security precautions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Live Voice Models and ChatGPT Work Agent
items.summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-Live, a new family of voice models capable of simultaneous real-time listening and speaking, and launched ChatGPT Work, an agent designed to execute tasks across applications and files for workplace automation[1].

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Stolen Trade Secrets for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees alleging misappropriation of trade secrets to benefit OpenAI's consumer hardware efforts, marking a dramatic escalation in tensions between the companies[1][2].

items.title: Meta Builds First Canadian AI Data Center in Alberta
items.summary: Meta announced it will invest over $9.1 billion to build its first data center in Canada, located in central Alberta, to support computing capacity for the global AI boom[1][4].

items.title: Meta Releases Muse Image and Muse Spark 1.1 Models
items.summary: Meta launched its image-generation model Muse Image alongside a new AI detection tool that failed to identify cropped versions of its own AI-generated images, and released Muse Spark 1.1 as part of its new Muse Spark family[1][6].

items.title: US Lifts Export Controls on Anthropic's Advanced AI Model
items.summary: The US government lifted export controls on one of Anthropic's most advanced AI models, ending a weeks-long ban tied to cybersecurity concerns, while China reported security vulnerabilities in Claude Code[8][2].

items.title: OpenAI Restricts GPT-5.6 Sol Release to Trump-Approved Customers
items.summary: OpenAI limited the release of its new AI model GPT-5.6 Sol to customers approved by President Donald Trump's administration during a cybersecurity review, while also rolling out Terra and Luna models[4][7].

items.title: South Korean Giants Plan $518B Chipmaking Hub for AI Demand
items.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plan a combined $518 billion investment in a new computer chip manufacturing hub in South Korea to serve soaring AI demand, with SK Hynix forecasting a worst-ever memory supply shortage in 2027[1][4].

items.title: Nature Study Claims AI Models 'Remember' Rather Than Learn Chemistry
items.summary: A new study in a Nature sub-journal reveals that AI models predicting drug effectiveness primarily rely on recalling existing data and chemical similarities rather than learning specific chemical interactions[10].

items.title: Industry Trend: AI Race Shifts to Cheaper, Smarter Systems
items.summary: The AI industry is shifting focus from building bigger models to developing cheaper, smarter systems, with companies now selecting models based on task, cost, and control rather than just leaderboard rankings[6][7].

items.title: Tata Consultancy Services Bets on AI with 8,900 Engineers and Acquisitions
items.summary: Tata Consultancy Services is building a team of up to 8,900 forward-deployed engineers and seeking AI acquisitions, betting that AI will create new business opportunities rather than undermine outsourcing[1].

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Lays Off Over 50 Bethesda Staffers as Microsoft Continues Studio Cuts
items.summary: Bethesda has lost more than 50 employees in recent Xbox layoffs, while Obsidian Entertainment saw approximately 25% of its staff cut as Microsoft pivots the studio to focus on Fallout instead of Avowed.

items.title: Doom: Revelations Expansion Launches Despite Xbox Layoff News
items.summary: id Software released the new Doom expansion 'Revelations,' showcasing the developer's signature style, though the launch was overshadowed by reports that the studio is also facing layoffs under Microsoft.

items.title: Nintendo Ends Mobile Support for Mario Kart Tour and Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis
items.summary: Nintendo announced it will end support for Mario Kart Tour in September, while Square Enix confirmed Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis will reach end-of-service on October 6, 2026.

items.title: Pokémon Champions Competitive Game Releases This Week on Switch
items.summary: Competitive Pokémon players can launch into 'Pokémon Champions' this week on Nintendo Switch, with a mobile version scheduled for later in 2026.

items.title: Major Switch 2 Announcements Include Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth and Oblivion Remastered
items.summary: New titles announced for Nintendo Switch 2 include Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth, Oblivion Remastered, Blue Prince, and Minishoot Adventures, alongside a surprise upgrade for Xenoblade Chronicles X.

items.title: Animal Crossing: New Horizons 3.0 Update Released Early for Switch Users
items.summary: A surprise early release of the massive 'Animal Crossing: New Horizons' 3.0 update is now available for players on Switch and Switch 2, delivering the first new content in years.

items.title: Kirby Air Riders for Switch 2 Hits First Price Drop at 33% Off
items.summary: The fast-paced racing game 'Kirby Air Riders' is now on sale for $50 (originally $70) at Amazon, marking its first discount of 33%.

items.title: Star Fox Remake Emerges as Switch 2's Most Impressive Visual Showcase
items.summary: Nintendo's remake of the N64 game 'Star Fox 64,' released as a Switch 2 exclusive on June 25, is being hailed as the platform's most impressive visual demonstration yet.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI and Two Former Employees for Trade Secret Theft
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing OpenAI and two ex-employees of stealing trade secrets to develop upcoming consumer AI hardware, marking a dramatic escalation in tensions between the two companies.

items.title: EU Charges Meta's Instagram and Facebook with Breaching Tech Rules Over 'Addictive Design'
items.summary: The European Commission charged Meta on Friday, demanding significant changes to autoplay and infinite scroll features on Facebook and Instagram to address 'addictive design,' with fines looming if changes aren't made.

items.title: SK Hynix Forecasts Worst-Ever Global Memory Supply Shortage in 2027
items.summary: SK Hynix CEO Kwak Noh-jung warned that the global memory industry faces its worst supply shortage in 2027, with demand expected to outstrip production capacity well into the next decade despite capacity expansions.

items.title: Apple Raises MacBook and iPad Prices Due to Rising Memory and Chip Costs
items.summary: Apple increased prices on popular products like the MacBook and iPad, citing rising costs for memory and storage chips driven by industry demand.

items.title: Waymo Expands Driverless Ride Service to Four New U.S. Markets
items.summary: Waymo announced it will begin offering driverless rides in four additional U.S. markets as its autonomous vehicle expansion accelerates.

items.title: Microsoft Cuts 3,200 Jobs in Xbox Division Amid Restructuring
items.summary: Microsoft is eliminating approximately 3,200 jobs across its Xbox video game business as it restructures the struggling division and shifts resources toward other initiatives.

items.title: Fiat Launches Tiny European EV in the U.S. with Low Price Tag
items.summary: Fiat is introducing an 8-foot-long electric vehicle to the U.S. market with a low price, offering up to 46 miles of range from a 5.4-kWh battery, though availability may face limitations.

items.title: Surgeons Perform First Surgery Using Human-Looking Robots
items.summary: Medical teams successfully performed surgery for the first time using robots designed to resemble humans, marking a breakthrough in robotic surgical technology.

items.title: China Recaptures First Stage of Rocket After Launch, Marking Space Program Breakthrough
items.summary: China successfully recovered the first stage of a rocket following a launch, representing a significant advancement in its reusable space launch capabilities.

items.title: Activist Investor Elliott Builds Large Stake in CCC Intelligent Solutions as Firm Explores Sale
items.summary: Elliott Investment Management has acquired a significant stake in software firm CCC Intelligent Solutions, which is reportedly exploring a potential sale.
